title: Understanding Agentic AI in Clinical Development
summary: Earlier this year, my colleague Matt Harrington suggested that clinical leaders should stop asking whether their organizations have an “AI strategy”, not because strategy doesn’t matter, but because the question often produces the wrong conversation.

title: BlossomHill eyes IPO as fertilizer to grow taller in crowded cancer fields

title: Jasper finds new purpose in merger with Kira, while offloading Tavneos rival to Mirador
summary: Jasper Therapeutics is being reborn via a merger with Nasdaq-listed immune-mediated disease biotech Kira Pharmaceuticals.

title: 5 emerging biotech hubs
summary: Boston. San Francisco. Beijing? The global biotech industry has long been concentrated in a handful of cities. But which emerging hubs could become dominant forces in biotech in the decades ahead?

title: Chutes & Ladders—Cue lines up new CMO and research head
summary: Cue Biopharma is tapping in a new chief medical officer and head of R&D in Dominic Borie, M.D., Ph.D., a physician-scientist with over two decades of industry experience.

title: Abbott Q2 led by strong device, diagnostic sales as it boosts profit forecast
summary: Abbott turned in a stronger-than-expected second quarter, allaying market concerns that subsidy cuts to the Affordable Care Act by the Trump administration would hurt medtech firms.

title: GSK axes key cough drug candidate after phase 3 data end blockbuster hopes
summary: GSK has ended development of camlipixant in chronic cough after delivering mixed phase 3 data, dealing a major blow to a potential blockbuster that analysts saw as the key 2026 catalyst for the drugmaker.

title: Dimerix snaps up Mission’s stalled, phase 2-ready kidney disease candidate in $292M deal
summary: Dimerix has bought an acute kidney injury drug from Mission Therapeutics, paying $5 million upfront and committing up to $287 million in milestones for the phase 2-ready asset.

title: Biotronik Neuro snaps up spinal cord stimulation portfolio from Soin Neuroscience for next-gen pain project
summary: Biotronik Neuro has acquired a portfolio of spinal cord stimulation patents from the Ohio-based medical device company Soin Neuroscience as the pair look to create “a next-generation spinal cord stimulation program.”

title: Takeda’s zasocitinib proves ‘top-notch’ for difficult-to-treat psoriasis in phase 3 update
summary: Takeda has unveiled more data from its phase 3 trials of zasocitinib, demonstrating that the once-daily pill was able to clear or almost clear scalp psoriasis in 75% of patients.

title: Nobel Laureate Jennifer Doudna enters AI protein design arena
summary: Jennifer Doudna, Ph.D., the Nobel Laureate best known for co-inventing the CRISPR system that has become the foundation for gene-editing, is dipping her toes into a new, buzzy field: AI-powered protein design.

title: Why Axiom hopes it’s ‘solved 2 major potholes’ that have hurt other stem cell biotechs
summary: When Axiom's stem cell therapy demonstrated a 0% mortality rate over two years in potentially fatal newborn neurological diseases this week, the biotech's leadership saw a broader validation for their approach to targeting the brain.

title: Racing Takeda, InnoCare reports TYK2 inhibitor win in phase 2 vitiligo trial
summary: A phase 2 trial of InnoCare Pharma’s TYK2 inhibitor soficitinib in nonsegmental vitiligo has hit its primary endpoint, clearing the Chinese biotech to advance into the phase 3 part of the study.

title: Lilly buys AtaiBeckley for $2.8B upfront to challenge J&J for psychedelic mental health market
summary: Eli Lilly has struck a deal to buy AtaiBeckley for $2.8 billion upfront, continuing its takeover spree to move into a psychedelic mental health sector targeted by companies including Johnson & Johnson.

title: Vogenx joins the IPO queue to fund phase 2-stage metabolic drug
summary: Vogenx has joined the growing queue for the Nasdaq, as the metabolic biotech eyes an IPO to fund its SGLT1 inhibitor.

title: Operating at the speed of science: The new medical affairs playbook
summary: As AI, data and rising HCP expectations redefine medical affairs, leaders need a more connected model for turning insight into action.

title: Why Commercial Evidence Is the Next Breakthrough in Pharma Intelligence
summary: Clinical teams have long used real world evidence to understand how a drug works. Now commercial teams can mine evidence from unstructured data to drive action.

title: Access: A key driver of non-opioid pain management adoption

title: Why modern clinical trials need a finance-first strategy
summary: As trial complexity rises, finance modernization is becoming a strategic imperative for speed, site trust and operational resilience.

title: Novartis gains full FDA nod for Fabhalta in kidney disorder IgAN
summary: The FDA has converted its accelerated approval for Novartis' Fabhalta into a full blessing to treat patients with immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN). The drug is endorsed to slow kidney function decline in adults at risk of disease progression.

title: Regulatory tracker: BI's Jascayd nod in Europe; Novo's India MASH green light

title: Novo, Alvotech face continued FDA scrutiny at plants tied to recent FDA rejections
summary: From Indiana to Iceland, the FDA this week disclosed concerns raised during recent visits to a pair of manufacturing sites with troubled regulatory histories.

summary: Fierce Pharma is launching the weekly Oral GLP-1 Tracker, leveraging analysts’ notes and IQVIA data to monitor prescription trends and shifting market shares as Novo Nordisk's Wegovy pill and Eli Lilly's Foundayo vie to become the go-to oral weight-loss solution for millions.

title: Huahai poaches quality chief from Hengrui amid FDA manufacturing citations

title: Vanda shifts Nereus marketing into high gear with Schumacher IndyCar sponsorship

title: Bipartisan Senate bill seeks to build vigilance around foreign companies making drugs in US
summary: A bipartisan bill reintroduced by three senators aims to increase transparency around foreign influence on the United States’ drug supplies, marking a potential first step to imposing restrictions.

title: Merck scores at FDA as Lipfendra becomes world's first oral PCSK9 treatment
